rules.rule_text: Preclinical assessment of antiarrhythmic agents must include both in vitro (multicellular tissues and/or single myocytes) and in vivo (appropriate animal models) studies to determine mechanism of action.
rules.verbatim_quote: A.A. medicinal products require a detailed and sophisticated preclinical assessment, both in vitro in multicellular tissues and / or single myocytes and in vivo in appropriate animal models in order to determine their mechanism of action.

rules.rule_text: Preclinical studies must evaluate electrophysiological properties (automaticity, conduction action potential duration, refractory period, receptors, pumps) and autonomic nervous system effects. Comparison with reference medicinal products is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: The studies should allow a precise evaluation of the electrophysiological properties of the compound with regard to automaticity, conduction action potential duration, refractory period of the action potential, receptors and pumps and its effects on the autonomic nervous system. Comparison with reference medicinal products will have to be made.

rules.rule_text: Proarrhythmic effect must be investigated carefully both in vitro and in vivo during preclinical assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: A proarrhythmic effect should be investigated carefully in vitro and in vivo.

rules.rule_text: All efficacy parameters used in clinical trials must be clearly defined in the protocol. Major efficacy endpoints are not necessarily linked and cannot reliably serve as surrogates for others.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any parameter used to assess efficacy in clinical trials should be clearly defined in the protocol. Major efficacy endpoints are not necessarily linked and cannot reliably serve as surrogates for others.

rules.rule_text: Sample size calculations must consider both efficacy and safety aspects. Statistical considerations must include a realistic risk estimate and arguments for acceptability of an increased risk/benefit ratio.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sample size calculations should be performed with respect to the efficacy criterion but should consider safety aspects as well. The statistical considerations should include a realistic estimate of the risk in the targeted population, and arguments for acceptability of an increased risk/benefit ratio should be presented.

rules.rule_text: Multiple lead ECGs (at least standard 12-lead) must be recorded with full analysis of automaticity, conduction (PR and QRS intervals), ventricular recovery period (QT, JT intervals), ST segment and T waves, both at rest and during exercise.
rules.verbatim_quote: Multiple lead electrocardiograms (at least the standard 12 lead ECG) should be recorded, with full analysis of effects on automaticity, conduction (PR and QRS intervals), ventricular recovery period (QT, JT intervals), the ST segment and T waves, both at rest and during exercise.

rules.rule_text: Holter monitoring must be considered for a minimum of 24 hours before and during antiarrhythmic therapy.
rules.verbatim_quote: Continuous long term ECG recording e.g. Holter monitoring should be considered for a minimum of 24 hours before as well as during antiarrhythmic therapy.

rules.rule_text: Invasive electrophysiological studies (EPS) must evaluate effects on automaticity, conduction refractoriness, and arrhythmia induction. These studies must be limited to selected centres.
rules.verbatim_quote: Invasive electrophysiological studies should evaluate the effects of the antiarrhythmic on automaticity, conduction refractoriness and arrhythmia induction. ... electrophysiological studies of this type should be limited to selected centres.
rules.exceptions: Beta-receptor modulating drugs tend to give unreliable EPS results

rules.rule_text: For life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, the main efficacy criterion is immediate prolongation of life. Survival studies are mandatory if increased survival is claimed.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the treatment of life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, the main efficacy criterion is immediate prolongation of life. ... If increased survival is claimed as a result of treatment, survival studies are mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Mortality data must be reported regardless of the clinical claim. Patient characteristics regarding mortality data must be specified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If increased survival is not claimed, mortality data must be reported whatever the clinical claim and the patients characteristics regarding the mortality data must be specified

rules.rule_text: For major symptomatic ventricular arrhythmias and prevention of potentially life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, demonstration of significant morbidity and symptom reduction is sufficient, provided data do not support a reasoned suspicion of negative effect on life expectancy.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the treatment of major symptomatic ventricular arrhythmias and in the prevention of potentially life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, it may be sufficient to demonstrate a significant reduction of morbidity and symptoms as long as the provided data do not support a reasoned suspicion of a negative effect on life expectancy.
rules.exceptions: Clinical trials in high-risk patients needed to investigate potential negative effect

rules.rule_text: For maintaining sinus rhythm in supraventricular arrhythmias, mortality data are required as an endpoint if the targeted population is not symptomatic.
rules.verbatim_quote: if the claimed indication is to maintain sinus rhythm in patients with supraventricular arrhythmias, mortality data are required as an endpoint if the targeted population is not symptomatic.

rules.rule_text: Safety data in patients with and without decreased left ventricular function and/or associated ventricular arrhythmias will be requested to eliminate a significant proarrhythmic effect.
rules.verbatim_quote: In any case, safety data in patients with and without decreased left ventricular function and/or associated ventricular arrhythmias will be requested in order to eliminate a significant proarrhythmic effect.

rules.rule_text: For non-life-threatening arrhythmias, safety is the paramount consideration. For life-threatening arrhythmias, significant safety risks may be acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: For life-threatening arrhythmias the primary concern is for efficacy. Inconvenience and even significant safety risks may be acceptable in order to control the rhythm disorder. ... For arrhythmias which are not life-threatening, safety is the paramount consideration.

rules.rule_text: Arrhythmogenic action must be addressed: antiarrhythmics can cause worsening of pre-existing arrhythmia(s) and/or provoke new forms. Prolonged and extensive experience is necessary to assess proarrhythmic effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: Arrhythmogenic action. A.A. medicinal products are known to cause worsening of pre-existing arrhythmia(s) and/or to provoke new form(s) of arrhythmia(s). ... Prolonged and extensive experience is necessary to assess this specific side effect.

rules.rule_text: The method for monitoring proarrhythmic activity must be identified (e.g., plasma level, QRS and/or QT prolongation).
rules.verbatim_quote: The way by which the activity of the drug can be most suitably monitored must be indicated (e.g. plasma level, QRS and/or QT prolongation).

rules.rule_text: Effects on cardiovascular function (automaticity, conduction, contractility, vascular effects) must be accurately explored both in the acute and chronic phase, especially in high-risk groups.
rules.verbatim_quote: Effects on cardiovascular function (automaticity, conduction, contractility, vascular effects). This should be accurately explored, by appropriate methods both in the acute phase and the chronic phase. This is particularly important in patients who belong to high risk groups

rules.rule_text: Special attention must be paid to patients with sinus node dysfunction, patients with pacemakers (pacing threshold), and patients with conduction defects.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be paid to patients with sinus node dysfunction, to patients with a pacemaker (pacing threshold) and patients with conduction defects.

rules.rule_text: PK and PD drug interactions must be specifically investigated. Clinically significant interactions at absorption and excretion level have been reported with antiarrhythmics.
rules.verbatim_quote: Interactions: special attention to pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interactions with other drugs. Clinically significant interactions at the absorption and excretion level have been reported with antiarrhythmic medicinal products and should therefore be investigated.

rules.rule_text: Initial studies of antiarrhythmics must be performed under close supervision of investigators experienced in cardiology and with broad experience in antiarrhythmic products, due to the small therapeutic index.
rules.verbatim_quote: Every precaution must be taken to minimise risks with the initiation of treatment in specialised hospital units under close surveillance by investigators experienced in cardiology and with broad experience in A.A. medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: PK studies must address: isoenzymes in drug metabolism, active metabolites, genetic influences on metabolism, dose-plasma level relationships, PK in risk groups (elderly, cardiac failure, hepatic/renal insufficiency), and both oral and parenteral forms.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be paid to identification of isoenzymes involved in drug metabolism and of active metabolites ... any evidence of genetic influence on metabolism ... pharmacokinetics in risk groups (elderly, cardiac failure, hepatic/renal insufficiency patients) ... dosage form; preferably both the oral and parenteral forms should be studied.

rules.rule_text: Patients with risk factors (metabolic/electrolyte disorders, cardiac failure, liver/renal insufficiency) must be enrolled in a priori stratified manner, as they influence dose-response and are highly representative of the target population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since patients with risk factors are highly representative for the ultimate target population, these patients should be enrolled in an a priori stratified manner.

rules.rule_text: Crossover design is not appropriate for arrhythmia studies as most arrhythmias are unstable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Most arrhythmias are unstable and the use of a cross-over design is not appropriate.

rules.rule_text: Randomized double-blind comparison with placebo is needed in main therapeutic studies unless considered unethical. Comparison with established drugs under randomized double-blind conditions is also necessary.
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomised double-blind comparison with placebo is needed, unless considered unethical. ... it should be necessary to compare the investigational compound under randomised, double-blind conditions with one or more established drugs of various types in order to define its place in therapy.
rules.exceptions: Placebo comparison may be unethical for life-threatening arrhythmias

rules.rule_text: For oral antiarrhythmic agents, safety data on 300-600 patients for 6 months (or at least 100 patients for a minimum of one year) will be required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since oral A.A. agents are likely to be administered for a long period of time, generally safety data on 300 - 600 patients for 6 months (or at least 100 patients for a minimum of one year) will be required.
